reasoning: IDEXX experienced a massive post-earnings gap on November 3, jumping to $722.94 on strong earnings, raised full-year guidance, and analyst price target upgrades (most prominently to $700 and $775/$830). Since then, the stock has experienced four consecutive days of downward drift, closing at $708.45 on Friday, November 7 (which will serve as the threshold for the 2025-11-09 cutoff). The negative short-term momentum, coupled with a stock price still historically elevated after stepping up ~14% in one day (and slightly above some prominent $700 price targets), points to a continuing short-term fade or consolidation. The provided statistical model estimates a 0.39 probability of exceeding $708.45 by November 16 based on linear trend and historical seasonal weakness, and I adjust this back slightly towards the base rate (0.50). For dates further out in December and February, uncertainty increases and the overall upward drift of equities in general plus IDXX's fundamentally strong earnings and high targets (e.g. $830) pushes the longer-term probabilities back towards and slightly above 0.50.
